title: Ceratochilus insignis
intro: Ceratochilus insignis, also known as Indian snakeroot or Kavach, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which help in treating fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Ceratochilus insignis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ceratochilus insignis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is also used in the form of a paste or oil for topical applications.
